diversification.simpleMeaning: Putting things in different groups to stay safer
diversification.contextMeaning: Putting money in different places to lower risk
diversification.sentence: Daniel told her one word that made her relax: diversification.

spread.contextMeaning: To put money into many different places
spread.sentence: It means you spread your money across different places.

across.simpleMeaning: From one side to the other
across.contextMeaning: In many different parts or places
across.sentence: It means you spread your money across different places.

places.contextMeaning: Different things or accounts where money goes
places.sentence: It means you spread your money across different places.

put.contextMeaning: Add money or things into one place or choice
put.sentence: You do not put everything into one stock or one idea.

everything.sentence: You do not put everything into one stock or one idea.

if.simpleMeaning: When this happens, then that happens
if.contextMeaning: When one thing happens, the next thing may happen
if.sentence: If one part falls, another part may stay steady.

falls.sentence: If one part falls, another part may stay steady.

another.sentence: If one part falls, another part may stay steady.

stay.sentence: If one part falls, another part may stay steady.

steady.contextMeaning: Keeps the same and does not change much
steady.sentence: If one part falls, another part may stay steady.

remove.sentence: Diversification cannot remove all risk, but it can reduce the damage.

all.contextMeaning: Every part, with nothing left out
all.sentence: Diversification cannot remove all risk, but it can reduce the damage.

reduce.sentence: Diversification cannot remove all risk, but it can reduce the damage.

damage.contextMeaning: The harm caused by a loss
damage.sentence: Diversification cannot remove all risk, but it can reduce the damage.

learned.contextMeaning: Found out about ETFs and asset allocation
learned.sentence: Maria learned about ETFs, which are funds that hold many stocks or bonds at once.

etfs.sentence: Maria learned about ETFs, which are funds that hold many stocks or bonds at once.

funds.contextMeaning: Money groups that buy many stocks or bonds
funds.sentence: Maria learned about ETFs, which are funds that hold many stocks or bonds at once.

hold.simpleMeaning: Keep something in your hand or place
hold.contextMeaning: Have many stocks or bonds inside
hold.sentence: Maria learned about ETFs, which are funds that hold many stocks or bonds at once.

once.simpleMeaning: At one time; not many times
once.contextMeaning: All together in one group or moment
once.sentence: Maria learned about ETFs, which are funds that hold many stocks or bonds at once.

etf.simpleMeaning: A basket that holds many investments
etf.contextMeaning: One investment that holds many different stocks or bonds
etf.sentence: One ETF can give variety without choosing each company herself.

give.sentence: One ETF can give variety without choosing each company herself.

variety.contextMeaning: Many different choices in one place
variety.sentence: One ETF can give variety without choosing each company herself.

without.contextMeaning: Doing it with no need to pick every company
without.sentence: One ETF can give variety without choosing each company herself.

choosing.sentence: One ETF can give variety without choosing each company herself.

each.sentence: One ETF can give variety without choosing each company herself.

asset.contextMeaning: A thing like stocks, bonds, or cash
asset.sentence: Maria also learned about asset allocation, which means the mix of stocks, bonds, and cash.

allocation.simpleMeaning: How things are shared or placed
allocation.contextMeaning: How money is divided between different kinds of investments
allocation.sentence: Maria also learned about asset allocation, which means the mix of stocks, bonds, and cash.

mix.sentence: Maria also learned about asset allocation, which means the mix of stocks, bonds, and cash.

young.simpleMeaning: Not old; still early in life
young.contextMeaning: A person with many years before retirement
young.sentence: A young person saving for a far goal might choose more stocks.

choose.contextMeaning: Decide to use more stocks or more bonds and cash
choose.sentence: A young person saving for a far goal might choose more stocks.

near.contextMeaning: Close to the time of retirement
near.sentence: A person near retirement might choose more bonds and cash.

rule.contextMeaning: A plan Maria made for herself
rule.sentence: Maria wrote a simple rule for herself.

keep.sentence: First, keep an emergency fund in cash.

emergency.simpleMeaning: A very bad time when help is needed fast
emergency.contextMeaning: Money saved for sudden problems or surprises
emergency.sentence: First, keep an emergency fund in cash.

fund.simpleMeaning: Money kept together for a special use
fund.contextMeaning: A pile of money saved for later use
fund.sentence: First, keep an emergency fund in cash.

amounts.simpleMeaning: Small parts of something, not a lot
amounts.contextMeaning: Small pieces of money put in little by little
amounts.sentence: Second, invest small amounts for long goals.

goals.simpleMeaning: Things you want to do or get later
goals.contextMeaning: Things you want to reach in the future
goals.sentence: Second, invest small amounts for long goals.

third.contextMeaning: The next step after the first two steps
third.sentence: Third, do not change the plan every time she sees scary headlines.

plan.simpleMeaning: A way you decide to do something
plan.contextMeaning: The money steps you decided to follow
plan.sentence: Third, do not change the plan every time she sees scary headlines.

every.simpleMeaning: All of them, one by one
every.sentence: Third, do not change the plan every time she sees scary headlines.

sees.sentence: Third, do not change the plan every time she sees scary headlines.

scary.contextMeaning: Making the news feel bad or worrying
scary.sentence: Third, do not change the plan every time she sees scary headlines.

headlines.simpleMeaning: Big words at the top of news stories
headlines.contextMeaning: The main news titles people read first
headlines.sentence: Third, do not change the plan every time she sees scary headlines.

feelings.simpleMeaning: What you feel inside, like happy or sad
feelings.contextMeaning: Emotions that can change money choices
feelings.sentence: The last lesson was about feelings, because feelings can move money faster than facts.

facts.sentence: The last lesson was about feelings, because feelings can move money faster than facts.

everyone.sentence: People often buy when everyone is excited.

excited.simpleMeaning: Very happy and full of energy
excited.contextMeaning: Feeling happy and eager to buy
excited.sentence: People often buy when everyone is excited.

fear.simpleMeaning: A bad feeling when you think something is wrong
fear.contextMeaning: Worry that makes people want to sell
fear.sentence: Then they sell when fear spreads.

spreads.simpleMeaning: Goes to many places or people
spreads.sentence: Then they sell when fear spreads.

selling.sentence: That usually means buying high and selling low.

opposite.simpleMeaning: The other side or different choice
opposite.sentence: Maria decided to do the opposite.

set.simpleMeaning: Make something ready or start it
set.sentence: She set a monthly transfer on payday into a simple, diversified ETF.

monthly.sentence: She set a monthly transfer on payday into a simple, diversified ETF.

transfer.simpleMeaning: Move money from one place to another
transfer.sentence: She set a monthly transfer on payday into a simple, diversified ETF.

payday.sentence: She set a monthly transfer on payday into a simple, diversified ETF.

diversified.contextMeaning: With many different kinds of investments
diversified.sentence: She set a monthly transfer on payday into a simple, diversified ETF.

promised.simpleMeaning: Said you will do it for sure
promised.sentence: She promised herself to review it only a few times a year.

review.simpleMeaning: Look at again to check it
review.contextMeaning: Look at it again to check it
review.sentence: She promised herself to review it only a few times a year.

few.sentence: She promised herself to review it only a few times a year.

times.contextMeaning: How many times she checks it
times.sentence: She promised herself to review it only a few times a year.

dramatic.simpleMeaning: Big and exciting, like a show
dramatic.sentence: It was not dramatic, but it felt like a real plan.

become.sentence: Maria did not become an expert in one week.

expert.simpleMeaning: A person who knows a lot about something
expert.contextMeaning: A person who knows a lot about this
expert.sentence: Maria did not become an expert in one week.

stopped.contextMeaning: Did not keep feeling helpless anymore
stopped.sentence: But she stopped feeling helpless when prices rose.

feeling.sentence: But she stopped feeling helpless when prices rose.

helpless.contextMeaning: Unable to do anything to fix things
helpless.sentence: But she stopped feeling helpless when prices rose.

use.contextMeaning: Take cash and time and make them help
use.sentence: She learned to use cash for safety and investing for long-term growth.

safety.sentence: She learned to use cash for safety and investing for long-term growth.

long-term.contextMeaning: For a future that is far away
long-term.sentence: She learned to use cash for safety and investing for long-term growth.

respect.contextMeaning: Be careful and serious about risk
respect.sentence: She also learned to respect risk and to use time as a helper.

helper.contextMeaning: Something that makes time help you
helper.sentence: She also learned to respect risk and to use time as a helper.

follow.contextMeaning: Do the plan step by step
follow.sentence: A simple plan you can follow is often better than a perfect plan you quit.

better.contextMeaning: More useful than the perfect plan
better.sentence: A simple plan you can follow is often better than a perfect plan you quit.

perfect.sentence: A simple plan you can follow is often better than a perfect plan you quit.

quit.sentence: A simple plan you can follow is often better than a perfect plan you quit.

opening.contextMeaning: The start of the lesson or talk

practice.simpleMeaning: Do something again to learn it
practice.contextMeaning: A question used for learning and checking understanding
practice.sentence: [PRACTICE] QUESTION: What problem did Maria notice with her savings account?

what.simpleMeaning: Asking about a thing or idea
what.contextMeaning: Asking for the problem or the meaning of inflation
what.sentence: [PRACTICE] QUESTION: What problem did Maria notice with her savings account?

problem.simpleMeaning: Something is wrong and needs fixing
problem.contextMeaning: Something was wrong with Maria's savings account
problem.sentence: [PRACTICE] QUESTION: What problem did Maria notice with her savings account?

notice.contextMeaning: She saw that something was wrong with her account
notice.sentence: [PRACTICE] QUESTION: What problem did Maria notice with her savings account?

answer.simpleMeaning: What you say to a question
answer.contextMeaning: The reply explains that the money grew slowly
answer.sentence: ANSWER: It grew slowly while prices around her kept rising.

grew.contextMeaning: The money became more, but not very fast
grew.sentence: ANSWER: It grew slowly while prices around her kept rising.

while.simpleMeaning: At the same time as something else
while.contextMeaning: The money grew at the same time prices went up
while.sentence: ANSWER: It grew slowly while prices around her kept rising.

around.simpleMeaning: Near something or in the nearby places
around.contextMeaning: Prices in the places near her were going up
around.sentence: ANSWER: It grew slowly while prices around her kept rising.

kept.simpleMeaning: Did the same thing again and again
kept.contextMeaning: Prices did not stop going up
kept.sentence: ANSWER: It grew slowly while prices around her kept rising.

rising.contextMeaning: Prices were getting higher and higher
rising.sentence: ANSWER: It grew slowly while prices around her kept rising.

words.simpleMeaning: Small parts we use to talk and write
words.contextMeaning: The question asks for a very easy explanation
words.sentence: QUESTION: What is inflation in simple words?

buys.contextMeaning: Money can get fewer things than before
buys.sentence: ANSWER: It means money buys less because prices go up.

less.contextMeaning: Money can buy a smaller amount of things
less.sentence: ANSWER: It means money buys less because prices go up.

go.simpleMeaning: Move from one place to another
go.sentence: ANSWER: It means money buys less because prices go up.

needs.simpleMeaning: Things you must have or use soon
needs.contextMeaning: Saving is for things you will need soon
needs.sentence: ANSWER: Saving is for soon needs.

down.simpleMeaning: To a lower place or level
down.sentence: Investing is for long goals and can go up or down.

earning.simpleMeaning: Getting more money or more of something
earning.contextMeaning: The gains make more gains over time
earning.sentence: ANSWER: It is when gains start earning more gains over time.

over.contextMeaning: For a long time, as time passes
over.sentence: ANSWER: It is when gains start earning more gains over time.

result.contextMeaning: The bad thing that happens after something
result.sentence: ANSWER: It spreads risk, so one bad result hurts less.

hurts.contextMeaning: Makes the bad thing feel worse
hurts.sentence: ANSWER: It spreads risk, so one bad result hurts less.

closing.simpleMeaning: The last part at the end
closing.contextMeaning: The last part of the text
closing.sentence: [CLOSING] Maria did not become an expert in one week.
